title: 30-Day Foundation
subtitle: Build the discipline you lost
description: Thirty days. One challenge each day. No excuses, no shortcuts. This program is the starting line — whether you're rebuilding from zero or just getting back on track. By day 30, discipline won't be something you find. It'll be something you are.

days.challengeTitle: Make your bed the moment you wake up
days.challengeDescription: Before your phone, before coffee, before anything. Make your bed. It's the smallest possible win — and it proves you can follow through on something.

days.challengeTitle: No phone for the first 30 minutes of your day
days.challengeDescription: Your morning shapes your entire day. Protect the first 30 minutes. No scrolling, no checking, nothing. Just you and the day ahead.

days.challengeTitle: Drink 2 liters of water today
days.challengeDescription: Simple. Non-negotiable. Most men are chronically dehydrated and don't notice. Track it today — bottle by bottle.

days.challengeTitle: Write down 3 things you will do today — and do them
days.challengeDescription: Not a wish list. A contract with yourself. Write three specific things before 9am, then execute. No carry-overs.

days.challengeTitle: Go for a 20-minute walk — no headphones
days.challengeDescription: Just you and your thoughts. No podcast, no music. Walk and think. Let your mind process without input for once.

days.challengeTitle: Read 10 pages of a real book
days.challengeDescription: Not an article. Not a thread. A book. 10 pages minimum. If you don't have one, get one today — that's also part of the challenge.

days.challengeTitle: Cold shower — full minute
days.challengeDescription: Step in, turn it cold, stay for 60 seconds. It's uncomfortable. That's the point. You are practicing doing hard things on purpose.

days.challengeTitle: Do 50 pushups throughout the day
days.challengeDescription: Doesn't have to be all at once. 10 in the morning, 20 at lunch, 20 before bed. Just hit 50. Your body keeps score.

days.challengeTitle: No social media until after 6pm
days.challengeDescription: Your attention is your most valuable resource. Guard it for one full day. Notice what happens to your focus.

days.challengeTitle: Cook every meal you eat today
days.challengeDescription: No delivery, no takeout, no skipping meals. Prepare everything yourself. It's a form of self-respect.

days.challengeTitle: Clean and organize one space in your home completely
days.challengeDescription: Pick one room, one desk, one drawer — but finish it completely. Your environment reflects your mental state. Change one, change the other.

days.challengeTitle: Reach out to someone you've been meaning to contact
days.challengeDescription: Not a text, not a like. A real message or call to someone important. Relationships need maintenance. Don't be the person who only shows up in crisis.

days.challengeTitle: Journal for 15 minutes — no filter
days.challengeDescription: Write whatever is in your head. Problems, fears, goals, frustrations. Get it out of your head and onto paper. Clarity lives on the other side of honesty.

days.challengeTitle: Run or walk 5km
days.challengeDescription: Halfway through the program. Celebrate it by doing something your body will remember. Pace doesn't matter. Completion does.

days.challengeTitle: Spend 30 minutes on a skill you want to develop
days.challengeDescription: Something you've been putting off. A language, an instrument, a craft, a tool. 30 focused minutes. Show up for the person you're becoming.

days.challengeTitle: No complaints — for the entire day
days.challengeDescription: Every time you catch yourself about to complain — stop. Reframe or say nothing. This is one of the hardest days in the program. It's supposed to be.

days.challengeTitle: Do 100 bodyweight reps — your choice of exercise
days.challengeDescription: Pushups, squats, lunges, burpees — pick one or mix them. Hit 100 total. Track every rep. Your body is capable of more than you think.

days.challengeTitle: Wake up one hour earlier than usual
days.challengeDescription: Use that hour for yourself — not for work. Read, move, think, plan. Guard it. The person who owns their morning owns their day.

days.challengeTitle: Write a letter to your future self — 1 year from now
days.challengeDescription: Where do you want to be? What are you proud of? What did you stop doing? Write it honestly. Seal it somewhere and don't read it for a year.

days.challengeTitle: Cut out sugar completely for today
days.challengeDescription: No added sugar. No sweetened drinks. No dessert. One day of control proves that you have it.

days.challengeTitle: Do something that scares you slightly
days.challengeDescription: Not reckless. Just outside your comfort zone. A conversation you've been avoiding. A class you signed up for but never went to. A call you've been delaying. Do it.

days.challengeTitle: Spend 1 hour completely offline — no screens at all
days.challengeDescription: One hour. No phone, no laptop, no TV. Read, walk, sit, think. Prove you can exist without a screen.

days.challengeTitle: Write down everything you eat and drink today
days.challengeDescription: No judgement, no diet. Just awareness. Most men have no idea what they actually consume in a day. Know your inputs.

days.challengeTitle: Do something for someone else with no expectation of return
days.challengeDescription: Help a neighbor. Cook for someone. Handle a task for a family member without being asked. Generosity is a discipline too.

days.challengeTitle: Write down your top 3 values — then audit your week against them
days.challengeDescription: What do you actually value most? Honesty, health, family, focus? Write them. Then ask: did your actions this week match these values? Be ruthlessly honest.

days.challengeTitle: Do a 45-minute workout of your choice
days.challengeDescription: Gym, run, home workout — your call. 45 continuous minutes of movement. This close to the end, there are no more excuses.

days.challengeTitle: Eliminate your biggest time-wasting habit for one day
days.challengeDescription: You know what it is. The app, the show, the habit you do when you're avoiding something real. One day without it. See what fills the space.

days.challengeTitle: Plan your next 90 days
days.challengeDescription: One goal for your health. One goal for your work or finances. One goal for your relationships. Write the goal and one concrete next step for each. Direction before momentum.

days.challengeTitle: Tell someone about a struggle you haven't talked about
days.challengeDescription: One person you trust. Something real. Carrying things alone gets heavy. Saying it out loud is the first step to actually dealing with it.

days.challengeTitle: Reflect — write down who you were on day 1 vs who you are today
days.challengeDescription: Not a celebration, an honest assessment. What changed? What didn't? What are you going to carry forward? Thirty days ago you started. Today you finish. This is just the beginning.
